behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, capped at a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this can be caught inside one retry/backoff policy object by feeding consecutive failure counts and config. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required; fake time values are enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text does not require module handoff; it specifies the backoff formula itself.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure backoff policy function/object: attempt 1..N yields exponential delays and then clamps at maxDelay.

behaviors.description: Retry logic identifies a rate-limit response and computes the wait before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is often at the boundary between response parsing (HTTP layer) and retry decision logic. Integration-brake checks: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed if using in-process stub responses; (b) no AI output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP transport conditions, only correct interpretation of rate-limit responses.
behaviors.test_description: Wire response-mapping module + retry policy; feed representative 429/rate-limit payloads/headers and verify retry is triggered with the expected delay.

behaviors.description: After waiting, the client resends the original request and returns the successful result transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requires interaction of timer/backoff scheduler, request executor, and call() wrapper state. Unit-only tests can miss handoff bugs (request mutation/loss across retry boundary). Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network required; fake timers + stub transport reproduce failure; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real network behavior, only resend/return flow.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock and stub transport: first attempt returns rate-limit, second succeeds; assert same original request is resent and call() resolves with success payload.

behaviors.description: After max retries, client returns typed QuotaExhaustedError instead of a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when retry counter/loop termination and error-mapping modules interact. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network required; repeated stubbed rate-limit failures are sufficient; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because typed error mapping is internal control-flow behavior, not a real infra condition.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to always return rate-limit; configure maxRetries; assert final rejection is QuotaExhaustedError with expected typed fields, not generic Error.

behaviors.description: Full retry chain (initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking) terminates and never loops indefinitely before surfacing error.
behaviors.justification: EVIDENCE-ANCHOR: behavior explicitly spans multiple internal components (retry chain + quota tracking), so boundary failures are in module interaction/state propagation. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required with fake clock + stub transport; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because infinite-loop risk here is in internal retry state logic, not real infra primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Run call() with transport that keeps returning rate-limit; advance fake timers; assert finite number of attempts and terminal QuotaExhaustedError without unbounded scheduling.

behaviors.description: Concurrent async callers in the same process draw from one shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a shared-state handoff/concurrency behavior across callers and quota manager; single-function tests miss cross-module race/order bugs. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; in-process concurrency test catches it; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior scope is same-process shared state, not external infra semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple concurrent call() invocations against shared client/quota manager with controlled stub responses; assert aggregate token/attempt accounting uses one global bucket.

behaviors.description: Queued waiting requests are dispatched in original submission order.
behaviors.justification: EVIDENCE-ANCHOR: ordering guarantee is a queue-orchestration contract across async scheduler + request dispatcher modules. Unit tests of queue primitives alone can miss integration timing bugs. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required with fake timers/stub transport; (b)(c)(d) no AI/agent chain. Rejection gate: rejected System because FIFO correctness does not depend on real network/OS events in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Submit multiple requests quickly under quota pressure, control release via fake clock/stubs, and assert actual dispatch sequence matches submission sequence.
